theme: The future of corporate events — what AI can't replace

linkedin.text:
Everyone is talking about AI in events. Here's what it cannot replace.

I've been watching the AI wave wash through the events industry. The tools are impressive. Automated scheduling, personalized agendas, smart allocation of breakout sessions.

But after 200+ events and 16 years, here's what I know:

The vendor who drives 4 hours at 11pm because a bus broke down — AI didn't hire him. I did, 8 years ago.

The local fixer in Prague who knows which restaurant owner will open his private room for 50 people on 2 hours notice — AI can't text him. I can.

The moment when the CEO of a client company realizes the event you've been planning for 6 months isn't a budget item anymore — it's the highlight of their people's year — that realization doesn't come from a dashboard.

AI will make our industry more efficient. Better tools, faster responses, smarter logistics.

But the reason clients come back for the 4th, 5th, 8th time isn't the efficiency. It's the trust that when something goes wrong — and something always goes wrong — the right person is already on the phone fixing it.

That's still a human business.

What do you think AI will most transform in events? And what should stay human?

#EventIndustry #AI #FutureOfWork #CorporateEvents #EventProfessionals #UproductionEvents #MICE #EventTech #EventPlanning #LeadershipThoughts
linkedin.cta: DM or visit upe.co.il to start planning

→ Drive 4 hours at 11pm when a bus breaks down

AI will make us more efficient.
Trust is still human.

What should stay human in events? 👇
